mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the trigger activates, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term IC is negative over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as supply-side growth supports earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when inflation exceeds 3% annually, or returns are negative in >50% of rising INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and stable unemployment creates a favorable liquidity environment that sustains equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or forward returns turn negative despite the joint condition holding true.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annually confirms robust economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate profits align with macro trends.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following GDP growth above the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation increases trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction when inflation spikes above the threshold.
